data.title: Nickel Steady Amid Signs of Supply Tightness — Market Talk
data.description: Nickel is flat in Asian trade after gaining in the last session. More signs of supply tightness are emerging in Indonesia, say ANZ Research analysts in a note, citing Zhejiang Huayo Cobalt's plans to cut output at its Huafei project in Weda Bay by 50% on abnormal price volatility for sulphur, among other reasons. The project accounts for around 3% of global nickel supply, ANZ adds. This comes as the Middle East conflict drives up the price of the chemical used by Indonesian nickel producers for high-pressure acid leaching, ANZ says. The three-month nickel contract on the London Metal Exchange holds steady at $19,445 a metric ton. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Eco-Shop Marketing Faces Margin Risks if Cost Pressures Rise — Market Talk
data.description: Eco-Shop Marketing's earnings outlook could face uncertainty as its price-sensitive customer base may limit its ability to pass on higher costs if the Mideast conflict drags on, Maybank IB analyst Jade Tam says in a note. Eco-Shop has secured about 40% of its product supply for the next six months, helping to cushion near-term supply disruptions, but cost pressures may build if the Iran conflict persists, she says. Ongoing store expansion and a gradual recovery in same store sales should continue to support growth, she reckons, noting that the company plans to open about 100 new stores in fiscal 2027. Maybank cuts Eco-Shop's target price to 1.50 ringgit from 1.80 ringgit and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 2.3% higher at 1.31 ringgit.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Chicago wheat hits near two-year high on dryness concerns
data.description:
Chicago wheat futures hit their highest point in nearly two years on Wednesday, supported by concerns over dryness in the U.S. winter wheat belt and strong demand.

Corn rose and hovered near a two-month high, buoyed by strong export demand and weather concerns in the U.S. corn belt. Soybeans also climbed.

FUNDAMENTALS

* The most-active wheat contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) was up 0.11% at $6.58-1/2 a bushel, as of 0051 GMT.

* Soybeans rose 0.21% to $11.91-3/4 a bushel, while corn climbed 0.05% to $4.75-3/4 a bushel.

* Rainy weather in the U.S. Plains wheat belt could alleviate some of the drought stress on crops, but some areas may have already experienced yield loss. Upcoming rain is forecast to miss other dry areas.

* A report released on Tuesday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture's attaché in Australia projected the country's 2026/27 wheat harvest at 29 million metric tons, down 19% from the prior year.

* Saudi Arabia's main state buying agency, the General Food Security Authority (GFSA), said on Monday that it bought 985,000 tons of wheat in an international tender.

* Corn prices gained support from overly wet weather in the U.S. corn belt and robust export demand. Expectations of reduced planting due to high fertiliser costs have also supported corn futures.

* Early U.S. soybean and corn planting has been making good progress, though storms expected in the U.S. Midwest could delay seeding in some areas.

* Midwest rains are expected to taper by the middle of the week, so farmers are unlikely to remain sidelined for long.

* In Brazil, soybean exports were seen reaching 15.87 million tons in April, down slightly from the previous week's estimate of 16.39 million tons, according to ANEC.

* Commodity funds were net buyers of CBOT wheat, corn, and soyoil, traders said on Tuesday. (CBOT/FUNDS)

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0900 EU Consumer Confid. Final Apr

1200 Germany CPI Prelim YY Apr

1200 Germany HCIP Prelim YY Apr

1230 US Durable Goods Mar

1230 US Housing Starts Number Mar

data.title: Australian shares slide ahead of key inflation data, banks and miners drag
data.description:
By Kumar Tanishk

Australian shares fell for a seventh straight session on Wednesday, dragged down by banks and miners, as investors awaited a key inflation report that could seal the case for further rate tightening.

The S&P/ASX 200 slid 0.5% to 8,666.80 by 0028 GMT, on track for its longest losing streak since mid‑June 2022, after closing 0.6% lower on Tuesday.

The first-quarter inflation report, due later in the day, is expected to show headline prices jumped 1.4% on surging energy costs and core inflation ran at 0.9%, pushing the annual trimmed mean to a Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) unfriendly 3.5%.

That will leave the RBA scant room to manoeuvre, with rates already at 4.1% and markets assigning a near 85% chance that any upside surprise locks in another hike next month.

"If inflation persists, then I would expect the Australian market to underperform its peers as we are basically the only economy lifting rates. At the same time, we are seeing those increased price pressures come from the Iran conflict," said Heath Moss, portfolio manager at HLM Investments.

Financials fell 0.3%, although top lender Commonwealth Bank of Australia rose 0.6%.

Higher rates may lift bank margins, but stretched valuations, rising bad‑debt risks and slowing loan growth could offset those benefits, even as strong capital buffers limit the downside, Moss said.

Miners lost 1.6% on lower iron ore prices, with BHP , Rio Tinto and Fortescue slipping between 1.3% and 2%.

Gold stocks eased 1% on weaker bullion prices, with Northern Star Resources down 1%.

Lending some support to the market, energy stocks edged up 0.8%, buoyed by firmer oil prices.

Oil major Woodside climbed as much as 1.8% after posting quarterly operating revenue ahead of analyst consensus.

In New Zealand, the S&P/NZX 50 index slid 0.3% to 12,729.28, heading for its fourth straight session of losses.

data.title: Key facts: Breztri Wins FDA Asthma Approval; AZN Trial Data Received
data.description:
• AstraZeneca won FDA approval for Breztri as asthma maintenance, citing Phase III lung‑function gains vs ICS/LABA and rapid onset; Breztri already approved for COPD. AZN stock dipped modestly.12
  • FDA confirmed receipt of safety data from AstraZeneca’s midstage mantle cell lymphoma trial with Paradigm Health as part of a pilot to monitor trial data in near real time.3

data.title: Key facts: Barclays pretax £2.8bn; £430m provision; guidance retained
data.description:
• Barclays Q1 2026: group income ~£8.16–8.2bn, pretax profit £2.8bn, RoTE 13.5%, EPS 14.1p, cost‑to‑income ~56%, CET1 ~14.1%, NII ex‑IB +12% to £3.4bn, LLPs £823m, £500m buyback.123
  • Barclays raised provisions by £105m to £430m for a UK car‑loan commission probe; stopped that financing in 2019. Bank keeps 2028 guidance and plans up to £500m buyback after current £1bn program.4
  • Barclays reaffirmed its 2026 guidance for cost-to-income ratio and return on tangible equity despite unspecified charges, maintaining the same published targets for those metrics through 2026.5

data.title: Key facts: BP Q1 $3.8B; splits into two; pauses buybacks; warns Q2 dip
data.description:
• BP Q1 2026 underlying RC profit $3.2B, reported profit $3.8B; net income $3.2B. Net debt $25.3B, capex $3.3B, stronger oil trading and refining margins.1
  • BP split into upstream and downstream under CEO Meg O’Neill, paused quarterly buybacks, declared a 8.32¢ dividend and targets at least 4% annual dividend growth.2
  • BP warns Q2 upstream production down on seasonal maintenance and Middle East disruptions; higher customer volumes may be offset by weaker midstream results. 2026 capex guidance set at $13–13.5bn.3
  • BP net debt rose to $25.3B in Q1 from $22.18B year-end. BP kept 2026 net-debt target $14–18B, 2026 capex guidance $13–13.5B and planned $9–10B divestment proceeds.4
  • BP shifts focus to boost oil and gas output, cut renewables spending, simplify its portfolio and speed production growth; activist Elliott Management disclosed a stake in BP.5
  • BP targets $4B cut in hybrid debt by 2027 to simplify its portfolio, cut costs and bolster its balance sheet—company-stated goal and timeline for traders.6
  • BP quarterly mean EPS expected $0.88 (range $0.54–$1.08). Mean EPS estimate rose ~52.9% in three months. Average price target $49.40 vs last close $45.97.7
  • BP (BP.) reports quarterly earnings this week. Traders should watch release timing, revenue, profit and any updates on oil production and refining margins.8
